title: Snapshot ID
body_excerpt:
When generating a snapshot, an ID is returned but it's not useful for future reference:
```
doctl compute droplet-action snapshot ######## --snapshot-name "Doctl-Test1"

```

```
doctl compute image delete 426###### -f
Error: DELETE https://api.digitalocean.com/v2/images/426######: 404 The resource you were accessing could not be found.
```

title: Wrong Disk size and number of VCPU's upon droplet creation
body_excerpt:
I don't know if anyone has noticed this, or is having the same issue, but upon the creation of a droplet using doctl on all of my Linux boxes, lately, I always seem to get the wrong Disk size and/or number of VCPU established with the droplet which causes the $5 (1GB droplet) to be $10/mo and the $10 (2GB droplet) to be $20/mo and so on and so forth.

For example every time I create a 1GB droplet I end up with 30GB of disk space as opposed to the 25GB of disk space associated with the online dashboard:
```
$ doctl compute d c test --size 1gb --region nyc1 --image freebsd-12-x64 --ssh-keys xxxxxxxx --ssh-keys xxxxxxxx --wait

ID           Name    Public IPv4        Private IPv4    Public IPv6    Memory    VCPUs    Disk    Region    Image                   Status    Tags    Features

title: node not deleted with kubectl delete
body_excerpt:
Yesterday I created a node pool with the parameters `min=1` and `max=2`. Since at the beginning they were 2 nodes, I decided to force deletion (instead of waiting automatic deletion), I did it with `kubectl delete nodes name-38wa7` where `name-38wa7` was the name of my alternate node.

You can see when you check on the command line that the node has been deleted:
```
$ kubectl get nodes
NAME              STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
name-38wam   Ready    <none>   15h   v1.16.6
```

However, today, just by chance I look into my user interface and to my surprise the other node was still up.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/901061/80591285-b8f57480-8a1d-11ea-824a-cc1694d7acd7.png)

Can you clarify why is this happening? As far as I understand, the second node

title: Error when on VPN
body_excerpt:
Attempting to authenticate with API token using 'doctl auth init' was not possible when on my VPN (Service:Private Internet Access. Server:Melbourne, Aus) it prompts for entry of the token, but after that it just spews thousands of lines of junk.
I saw cloudflare written somewhere amongst it all and after dropping the VPN, and authenticating with token, VPN can be used again.

Blasting a whole webpage, including images onto the stdout is not super helpful, could you maybe detect the use of CLI Client and change the behavior to a short, helpful message?

title: Top-level doctl package fails golint check
body_excerpt:
### Overview

The top-level `doctl` package fails the `golint` check.

### How to fix it

1. Clone this repo.
2. If you haven't already, install Go through their [official installer](https://golang.org/dl/).
3. If you haven't already, install the official `golint` tool by following the instructions outlined in their [repo](https://github.com/golang/lint).
4. Run `golint` on the top-level `doctl` package in this repo. To do that, you can run, `golint ./`, and it will report style errors within that package.
5. Fix the issue shown by `golint`. Some are easy enough, but others might require some context. Here are some tips for some of the current issues,
    1. The issue around the `ArgRedirectHttpToHttps` arg will require that you also update where that variable is used after you

doctl version 1.46.0-release
release 1.48.1 is available, check it out!
```

 - OS: <!-- E.g. macOS Catalina, Ubuntu 19.04, e

title: Fix the configuration removing command
body_excerpt:
**Describe the Issue:**

```
rm -rf $HOME/Library/Application Support/doctl
```

this is won't work as expected, because of the space in `Application Support`. It will be treated as two different arguments, and this command will completely remove `$HOME/Library/Application` directory if that exists on the user machine, without asking.

Should be quoted:
```
rm -rf "$HOME/Library/Application Support/doctl"
```

title: AppSpec JSON Schema
body_excerpt:
**What is the problem this feature would solve? Please describe.**
I would like to create a JSON app spec file and there is no JSON Schema to be found.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Please publish a JSON Schema somewhere in your documentation.

**Additional context**
Here is a Go module that I created to generate the AppSpec JSON Schema:

)

	s := jsonschema.Reflect(&godo.AppSpec{})
	b, _ := json.MarshalIndent(s, "", "  ")
	fmt.Print("AppS

title: doctl auth init -t TOKEN should not print token to stdout
body_excerpt:
**Replication Steps**

When using doctl the default behavior of token auth, is to print the token to stdout per `<redacted>`.
```
doctl auth init -t $TOKEN

Using token [<redacted>]

Validating token... OK
```

This behavior is undesirable as the default behavior because security. 

**Desired Behavior**
No tokens or sensitive information are sent to stdout.

**Actual Behavior**
A sensitive token is printed to stdout which can be inadvertedly leaked.

**Reasoning**
Security. When doctl is used in CI (or other scenarios), it results in tokens being written to stdout which is often stored in a log state, creating opportunity for tokens to be leaked, unccessarily.

It is a requirement to authenticate docker with the DO registry via doctl, so it's a critical use case.

I

title: doctl kubernetes cluster node-pool create mycluster123 --auto-scale --min-nodes 0 --name test --size s-1vcpu-2gb --max-nodes 1 --count 0 says the node pool needs at least min 1
body_excerpt:
In your documentation (https://docs.digitalocean.com/reference/doctl/reference/kubernetes/cluster/node-pool/create/) you write:

> The minimum number of nodes in the node pool when autoscaling is enabled
> Default: 0

wheres when I write following command:

Error: POST https://api.digitalocean.com/v2/kubernetes/clusters/123123-123123123-123123123-123123/node_pools: 422 (request "123-123-123-123") validation error: worker_node_pool_spec.min nodes must be greater than or equal to 1
```

title: functions: attributes generated by quick start are undocumented
doctl compute action list is SLOW
body_excerpt:
**Describe the Issue:**
`doctl compute action list --after=2025-04-11T00:00:00.00Z` is VERY slow ~5 minutes. (Note: this is for <1 day of actions at time of posting)

**Environment:**
 - doctl version: doctl version 1.124.0-release
 - OS: MacOS Sequoia Version 15.4 (24E248)
 - Installation method: Homebrew

**Additional Details:**
I can't post the `--trace` here, it's FAR too big. But, when I run it with `--trace` it shows that it's trying to recursively loop over every single paginated API request (Ex: `GET /v2/actions?page=126&per_page=200`). We have a LOT of actions; 52,292 according to the output. That means that it's making 262 asynchronous API requests. Even if I pass an `--action-type=power_off`, it still makes the same non-filtered request. I now realize I'm probably barking up the
